examples.title: Low-Level Call with Proper Error Handling
examples.description: Demonstrates the correct pattern for handling low-level .call() with failure checking
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates proper error handling with low-level calls. The key is to always check the boolean success return. Without this check, failed calls are silently ignored, which can lead to security vulnerabilities and lost funds.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - where n is the size of return data buffer

examples.title: Upgradeable Proxy Pattern with Delegatecall
examples.description: Shows how delegatecall enables upgradeable contract architecture
examples.explanation: This proxy pattern uses delegatecall to execute implementation code while preserving the proxy's storage. When V2.setValue is called via proxy, it writes to the proxy's storage (value and lastUpdated slots). Upgrading simply changes the implementation address, keeping all stored data intact.

examples.title: Reentrancy Prevention with CEI Pattern
examples.description: Demonstrates the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern to prevent reentrancy attacks
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the critical CEI (Checks-Effects-Interactions) pattern. The vulnerable version updates balance AFTER the external call, allowing recursive attacks. The correct version updates state BEFORE making the external call. The best practice uses a reentrancy guard (mutex) for additional safety.
